name: Canterbury Park Holding Corp
summary: Canterbury Park Holding Corporation operates the Canterbury Park Racetrack and Casino in Shakopee, Minnesota, approximately 20 miles southwest of Minneapolis. The company conducts pari-mutuel wagering on live thoroughbred and quarter horse races (seasonal, May–September) and year-round simulcasting, hosts unbanked card games (poker and table games) at an up to 80-table Casino, provides food and beverage services, and pursues real estate development on its approximately 260-acre property through subsidiaries Canterbury Park Entertainment LLC and Canterbury Development LLC.

products.name: Pari-Mutuel Wagering (Live Racing)
products.description: Seasonal live thoroughbred and quarter horse racing with pari-mutuel wagering, hosted May through September at the Racetrack.

products.name: Simulcast Wagering
products.description: Year-round wagering on horse races from out-of-state and international tracks televised simultaneously at the Racetrack.

products.name: Casino Card Games
products.description: Unbanked poker and table games including Blackjack, Three Card Poker, and others, operating up to 80 tables 24/7.

products.name: Food and Beverage
products.description: Concessions, catering, and event services provided at the Racetrack facility.

products.name: Real Estate Development
products.description: Real estate development operations conducted through Canterbury Development LLC on the company's Shakopee land holdings.

products.name: Advanced Deposit Wagering (ADW)
products.description: Online pari-mutuel wagering for Minnesota residents on out-of-state horse races via ADW providers, collecting a source market fee.

partnerships.partner_name: Minnesota Horsemen's Benevolent and Protective Association (MNHBPA)
partnerships.description: Annual agreements governing purse contributions and consent for simulcast operations at the Racetrack.
Annual agreements governing purse contributions for quarter horse racing operations.
Receives 1% of pari-mutuel handle and 10% of purse monies as required by Minnesota law.
